key_insight: The paper introduces the Patnaik-Pearson dimension, a new intrinsic-dimension estimator for data manifolds, and shows it aligns with HTSR/SETOL-style spectral analysis for neural network weight matrices with Pareto-type spectra.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical way to track how representation complexity changes across transformer layers, which can help diagnose compression, redundancy, and scaling behavior in large language models.
method: Define a new intrinsic-dimension measure inspired by TwoNN and spectral-tail methods.
Prove theoretical properties and derive links to HTSR/SETOL when the empirical spectral density follows a power law.
Apply the estimator to token embeddings and layerwise representations in BERT-base and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports both theoretical analysis and numerical experiments on BERT-base and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1, including the initial embedding manifold and its evolution through model layers.
watch_item: The approach depends on estimating manifold dimension from finite, noisy representations and on assumptions like power-law spectral behavior, so results may be sensitive to model, layer, and estimator settings.
paper_title: Patnaik-Pearson intrinsic dimension for internal representations of neural networks
